name: Ingushetia (Russia)

colors.symbolism: purity of thought and intentions, the brightness of the future
nature, prosperity, and Islam, the predominant faith of the Ingush
the difficult historical struggle of the Ingush people for survival

description: A white field bordered above and below by narrow green horizontal stripes, charged in the centre with a red ancient solar emblem of curved arms.
history: The flag was first adopted on 15 February 1994 by the People's Assembly of the Republic of Ingushetia, with a re-confirmation in later law. The solar emblem is drawn from medieval Ingush tower architecture and folk symbolism.

features.symbolism: the white central field stands for purity of thought and intentions; the green bands represent nature, prosperity and Islam; the proportions place the white field over four-fifths of the height, framed by narrow green stripes.
an ancient Ingush solar emblem (a cross of three rays per arm radiating from a central disc); represents the eternal motion of the Sun and the resilience of the Ingush nation.
